```xml
		<outlook365:send-message doc:name="Send message" doc:id="71da69e5-6ec3-4fe1-beec-7ef4ce625527" config-ref="outlook365_client_credentials" userId="88f176b2-84ca-4cfe-828c-954db0096efa" messageId="AAMkADg5NjIxODNkLWRkNzAtNGFkNC04YjUwLWFjOTEyMzg0YzA5NgBGAAAAAADzzlN7RptORYu7QgvFkvQJBwCthX8I2XTFT5_USx5R95fIAAAAAAEPAACthX8I2XTFT5_USx5R95fIAAFXpn5uAAA="/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="f6f311e4-92e6-4eff-93b9-e3f0bd1d346c" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  From a web browser, test the application by entering
    `[http://localhost:8083/send-message](http://localhost:8083/send-message)`.

## Send Mail

This Mule flow sends a message without creating a draft message first.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Send mail**  
  Sends the message specified in the request body
- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Send mail** operation

![Studio Flow for the Send Mail operation](_images/send-mail.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tableau-specialist="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist" xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email"
	  xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:outlook365="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365" xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365/current/mule-outlook365.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist/current/mule-tableau-specialist.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="7b5e348d-2f1c-4a09-b73a-7b1f84d89060" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8083" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<outlook365:outlook365-config name="outlook365_client_credentials" doc:name="Outlook365 Outlook365 Config" doc:id="ab1353ac-79b8-4718-a96c-a2c9e9d1d996" >
		<outlook365:oauth-client-credentials-connection >
			<outlook365:oauth-client-credentials clientId="${oauth-client-credentials.clientId}" clientSecret="${oauth-client-credentials.clientSecret}" tokenUrl="${oauth-client-credentials.tokenUrl}" scopes="${oauth-client-credentials.scopes}" />
		</outlook365:oauth-client-credentials-connection>
	</outlook365:outlook365-config>

	<flow name="send-mail" doc:id="8c2eb7ec-b811-4598-bd84-8cdc5453563c" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="290e3637-9b48-421b-bfb1-2b6fd717bbf5" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/send-mail"/>
		<outlook365:send-mail doc:name="Send mail" doc:id="1179a8b1-faca-42a1-8728-4398236f884e" config-ref="outlook365_client_credentials" userId="88f176b2-84ca-4cfe-828c-954db0096efa" requestBodyContentFormat="MIME">
			<outlook365:message ><![CDATA[RnJvbTogU2hhcmVwb2ludCBUZWFtcyA8c2hhcmVwb2ludC10ZWFtc0BtdWxlY29ubmVjdG9yLm9ubWljcm9zb2Z0LmNvbT4KVG86IE11bGUgVGVzdCA8bXVsZXRlc3RAbXVsZXNvZnQuY29tPgpTdWJqZWN0OiBUZXN0IE91dGxvb2szNjUgY29ubmVjdG9yIC0gcGxlYXNlIGlnbm9yZQpEYXRlOiBTdW4sIDI4IEZlYiAyMDIxIDA3OjE1OjAwICswMDAw]]></outlook365:message>
		</outlook365:send-mail>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="ca16b61a-efda-45b6-aae9-f9cf0fc237eb" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  From a web browser, test the application by entering
    `[http://localhost:8083/send-mail](http://localhost:8083/send-mail)`.

## Create Reply All to Message

This Mule flow creates a reply-all message that is saved in the Drafts folder. The message can then
be sent using the **Send message** operation.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Create reply all to message**  
  Creates a draft to reply to the sender and all the recipients of the specified message
- **Transform Message**  
  Outputs the results of the **Create reply all to message** operation in JSON format
- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Create reply all to message** operation

![Studio Flow for the Create Reply All to Message operation](_images/create-reply-all-to-message.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tableau-specialist="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist" xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email"
	  xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:outlook365="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365" xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365/current/mule-outlook365.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist/current/mule-tableau-specialist.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="98f35d79-9083-46d1-b1a5-27f954c8b437" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8083" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<outlook365:outlook365-config name="outlook365_client_credentials" doc:name="Outlook365 Outlook365 Config" doc:id="a5a23cc1-c8ec-400c-b2be-9590ebcf49ac" >
		<outlook365:oauth-client-credentials-connection >
			<outlook365:oauth-client-credentials clientId="${oauth-client-credentials.clientId}" clientSecret="${oauth-client-credentials.clientSecret}" tokenUrl="${oauth-client-credentials.tokenUrl}" scopes="${oauth-client-credentials.scopes}" />
		</outlook365:oauth-client-credentials-connection>
	</outlook365:outlook365-config>

	<flow name="create-reply-all-to-message" doc:id="2584d71b-c2fb-4a8d-8c79-957ca4900e06" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="02424960-2abd-4c16-8caa-5d9c233189d3" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/create-reply-all"/>
		<outlook365:create-reply-all-to-message doc:name="Create reply all to message" doc:id="2cd08725-03a6-4945-98c0-3cfa559a16c8" config-ref="outlook365_client_credentials" userId="88f176b2-84ca-4cfe-828c-954db0096efa" messageId="AAMkADg5NjIxODNkLWRkNzAtNGFkNC04YjUwLWFjOTEyMzg0YzA5NgBGAAAAAADzzlN7RptORYu7QgvFkvQJBwCthX8I2XTFT5_USx5R95fIAAAAAAEJAACthX8I2XTFT5_USx5R95fIAAFD3jMsAAA=">
			<outlook365:reply ><![CDATA[#[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
{
  "blabla": "sssscomment-value"
}]]]></outlook365:reply>
		</outlook365:create-reply-all-to-message>
		<ee:transform doc:name="Transform Message" doc:id="43f037d7-2d48-42e1-b504-9ffda49b3175" >
			<ee:message >
				<ee:set-payload ><![CDATA[%dw 2.0
output application/json
---
payload]]></ee:set-payload>
			</ee:message>
		</ee:transform>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="3bdce785-d6d3-496b-8f8c-b2c7460293a0" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  From a web browser, test the application by entering
    `[http://localhost:8083/create-reply-all-to-message](http://localhost:8083/create-reply-all-to-message)`.

## Reply All to Message

This Mule flow creates a reply-all message, which is sent without creating it first in the Drafts
folder using the **Create message** operation.

This example uses the following operations:

- **HTTP Listener**  
  Accepts data from HTTP requests
- **Reply all to message**  
  Replies to all recipients of a message
- **Logger**  
  Shows the HTTP response from the **Reply all to message** operation

![Studio Flow for the Reply All to Message operation](_images/reply-all-to-message.png)

### XML for This Example

Paste this code into the Studio XML editor to quickly load the flow for this example into your Mule
app:

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

<mule xmlns:tableau-specialist="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist" xmlns:email="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email"
	  xmlns:ee="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core"
	  xmlns:http="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http" xmlns:outlook365="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365" xmlns:salesforce="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/salesforce" xmlns="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core" xmlns:doc="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/documentation" xmlns:xsi="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema-instance" xsi:schemaLocation="http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/core/current/mule.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365 http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/outlook365/current/mule-outlook365.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/http/current/mule-http.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/ee/core/current/mule-ee.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/email/current/mule-email.xsd
http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist http://www.mulesoft.org/schema/mule/tableau-specialist/current/mule-tableau-specialist.xsd">
	<http:listener-config name="HTTP_Listener_config" doc:name="HTTP Listener config" doc:id="98f35d79-9083-46d1-b1a5-27f954c8b437" >
		<http:listener-connection host="0.0.0.0" port="8083" />
	</http:listener-config>

	<outlook365:outlook365-config name="outlook365_client_credentials" doc:name="Outlook365 Outlook365 Config" doc:id="a5a23cc1-c8ec-400c-b2be-9590ebcf49ac" >
		<outlook365:oauth-client-credentials-connection >
			<outlook365:oauth-client-credentials clientId="${oauth-client-credentials.clientId}" clientSecret="${oauth-client-credentials.clientSecret}" tokenUrl="${oauth-client-credentials.tokenUrl}" scopes="${oauth-client-credentials.scopes}" />
		</outlook365:oauth-client-credentials-connection>
	</outlook365:outlook365-config>

	<flow name="reply-all-to-message" doc:id="74d00afa-c238-4a4a-90d8-c7639dd1bb2b" >
		<http:listener doc:name="Listener" doc:id="545d0bae-9e20-48f6-9dd5-4a36ecade538" config-ref="HTTP_Listener_config" path="/reply-all"/>
		<outlook365:reply-all-to-message doc:name="Reply all to message" doc:id="44693b84-7bba-4f61-a5f2-481c05d8a3d8" config-ref="outlook365_client_credentials" userId="88f176b2-84ca-4cfe-828c-954db0096efa" messageId="AAMkADg5NjIxODNkLWRkNzAtNGFkNC04YjUwLWFjOTEyMzg0YzA5NgBGAAAAAADzzlN7RptORYu7QgvFkvQJBwCthX8I2XTFT5_USx5R95fIAAAAAAEJAACthX8I2XTFT5_USx5R95fIAAFD3jMsAAA="/>
		<logger level="INFO" doc:name="Logger" doc:id="e2e303d6-d17d-4940-910d-e9a95a2728b0" message="#[payload]"/>
	</flow>
</mule>
```

### Steps for Running This Example

1.  Verify that your connector is configured.
2.  Save the project.
3.  From a web browser, test the application by entering
    `[http://localhost:8083/reply-all-to-message](http://localhost:8083/reply-all-to-message)`.

- [Introduction to Anypoint Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/introduction-to-anypoint-connectors)

# Microsoft Outlook 365 Connector 1.3 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

> [!NOTE] Where possible, we changed noninclusive terms to align with our company value of Equality.
> We maintained certain terms to avoid any effect on customer implementations.

> [!NOTE] All permissions for this connector must be assigned under the Microsoft Graph API.

## Configurations

---

### Outlook365 Config

#### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Outlook365Config_OauthAuthorizationCode">OAuth v2.0 Authorization Code</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#Outlook365Config_OauthClientCredentials">OAuth v2.0 Client Credentials</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Connection types for this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID used to reference this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the amount of time that the consumer waits for a response before it times out.</p></td><td><p>60</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

#### Connection Types

##### OAuth v2.0 Authorization Code

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

###### Value Providers Required Permissions

The following tables show the required permissions to include as scopes to your connection for value
providers in the operation:

**Calendar value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.Read.Shared</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.Read.Shared</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**Event value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**User value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>User.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>User.Read.All</code>, <code>User.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.Read.All</code>, <code>Directory.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p>Not supported.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**Outlook timezone value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>User.Read</code>, <code>User.ReadBasic.All</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>User.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**Mail folder value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code>, <code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code>, <code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL for Microsoft Graph endpoints to access from the connector and from which all other endpoints are derived.</p></td><td><p><code>https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies the method to use to send the resulting token back to your app. This must be <code>query</code>.</p></td><td><p>query</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration (DEPRECATED)</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs authorization endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/authorize</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL of the service providerâs access token endpoint.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Resource owner ID to use with the authorization code grant type.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after receiving an access token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configuration for the HTTP listener that listens for requests on the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

##### OAuth v2.0 Client Credentials

The OAuth 2.0 client credentials grant gets access to web-hosted resources by using the identity of
the application.

###### Value Providers Required Permissions

The following tables show the required permissions to include as scopes to your connection for value
providers in the operation:

**Calendar value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.Read.Shared</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**Event value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**User value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>User.Read.All</code>, <code>User.ReadWrite.All</code>, <code>Directory.Read.All</code>, <code>Directory.ReadWrite.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**Outlook timezone value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>User.Read.All</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

**Mail folder value provider permissions**:

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code>, <code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

###### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Proxy configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ProxyConfiguration">Proxy Configuration</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a proxy for outbound connections.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Base URL for Microsoft Graph endpoints to access from the connector and from which all other endpoints are derived.</p></td><td><p><code>https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS configuration (DEPRECATED)</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">TLS</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures TLS. If using the HTTPS protocol, you must configure TLS.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures a reconnection strategy to use when a connector operation fails to connect to an external server.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client ID, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Client Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth client secret, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Service providerâs token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant}/oauth2/v2.0/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. This value defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the object store that stores data for each resource owner. If not configured, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Operations

## Accept Event

`<outlook365:accept-event>`

Accepts the specified event from a userâs calendar.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Accept Event Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Accepts information for the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Add Event Attachment

`<outlook365:add-event-attachment>`

Adds an attachment to an event. This operation limits the size of the attachment you can add to 3
MB.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that contains the added attachment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attachment For Event</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Attachment added to the event.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Add Message Attachment

`<outlook365:add-message-attachment>`

Adds an attachment to a message. This operation limits the size of the attachment you can add to 3
MB.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the message that contains the added attachment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that contains the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attachment For Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Attachment added to the message.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Cancel Event

`<outlook365:cancel-event>`

Cancels the specified event from a userâs calendar.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reason for cancelling the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN

- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

## Copy Message

`<outlook365:copy-message>`

Copies a message to a folder.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the previously created message entity.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Destination Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the destination folder.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Create Event

`<outlook365:create-event>`

Creates an event in a userâs default calendar or in a specified calendar.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Event to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Outlook Time Zone</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time zone for the start and end times in the response. Although this parameter is supported, it does not work for the <strong>Create Event</strong> operation due to the implementation of the Microsoft API.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Create Message

`<outlook365:create-message>`

Creates a draft of a new message in either JSON or MIME format.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message to create.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body Content Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></li><li><p>MIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type for the request payload.</p></td><td><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that creates the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Create Reply All To Message

`<outlook365:create-reply-all-to-message>`

Creates a draft to reply to the sender and all the recipients of the specified message in either
JSON or MIME format.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the message that replies to all.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body Content Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></li><li><p>MIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type for the request payload.</p></td><td><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reply all message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS

- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

## Create Reply To Message

`<outlook365:create-reply-to-message>`

Creates a draft of the reply to the specified message in either JSON or MIME format.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the reply message.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body Content Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></li><li><p>MIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type for the request payload.</p></td><td><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Reply message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Decline Event

`<outlook365:decline-event>`

Declines the specified event from a userâs calendar.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Decline Event Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Declines information for the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Delete Event

`<outlook365:delete-event>`

Removes the specified event from the calendar that contains the event.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete Message

`<outlook365:delete-message>`

Deletes a message from the specified userâs mailbox.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the message to delete.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>
