## Forward Event

`<outlook365:forward-event>`

Enables the organizer or attendee of a meeting event to forward the meeting request to a new
recipient.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Forward Event Properties</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Forward information for the event.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Get Event

`<outlook365:get-event>`

Gets the properties and relationships of the specified event object. Currently, this operation
returns event bodies in HTML only.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Outlook Time Zone</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time zone for the start and end times in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Outlook Body Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TEXT</p></li><li><p>HTML</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type of the event body returned.</p></td><td><p>HTML</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Get Event Attachment

`<outlook365:get-event-attachment>`

Reads the properties and relationships of an attachment that is attached to an event. You can append
the path segment `/$value` to get the raw contents of a file or item attachment.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that retrieves the attachment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attachment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the attachment retrieved from the event.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get Raw Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to get the raw content of the attachment or not.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Get Message

`<outlook365:get-message>`

Retrieves the properties and relationships of a message object.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the retrieved message.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TEXT</p></li><li><p>HTML</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The format of the body and uniqueBody properties to return.</p></td><td><p>HTML</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get MIME Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Flag that indicates whether to return the MIME content of the message or not.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Get Message Attachment

`<outlook365:get-message-attachment>`

Reads the properties and relationships of an attachment that is attached to a user message. You can
append the path segment `/$value` to get the raw contents of a file or item attachment.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the message that retrieves an attachment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attachment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the attachment that the message retrieves.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Get Raw Content</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether to get the raw content of the attachment or not.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## List Event Attachments

`<outlook365:list-event-attachments>`

Retrieves a list of attachment objects attached to an event. If not specified by the **Top**
parameter, the default page size is 300.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Event Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the event that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Message of <strong>Binary</strong> payload and <strong>Void</strong> attributes</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## List Events

`<outlook365:list-events>`

Gets a list of event objects from the userâs mailbox. The list contains single instance meetings and
series masters. Currently, this operation returns event bodies in only HTML. If not specified by the
**Top** parameter, the default page size is 300.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Calendars.Read</code>, <code>Calendars.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Outlook Time Zone</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Time zone for the start and end times in the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Outlook Body Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TEXT</p></li><li><p>HTML</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type of the event body returned.</p></td><td><p>HTML</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Calendar Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the calendar that holds the event.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. - OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN

- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## List Message Attachments

`<outlook365:list-message-attachments>`

Retrieves a list of attachment objects attached to a message. If not specified by the **Top**
parameter, the default page size is 300.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Read</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message that performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. ## List Messages

`<outlook365:list-messages>`

Gets the messages from the signed-in userâs mailbox, including the Deleted Items and Clutter
folders. If not specified by the **Top** parameter, the default page size is 300.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code>, <code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code>, <code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadBasic.All</code>, <code>Mail.Read</code>, <code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>O Data Query Params</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ODataQueryParams">O Data Query Params</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Additional query parameters or filters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Outlook Body Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>TEXT</p></li><li><p>HTML</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The format of the body and uniqueBody properties to return.</p></td><td><p>HTML</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>MIME type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Encoding</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Encoding of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Move Message

`<outlook365:move-message>`

Moves a message to another folder within the specified userâs mailbox. This creates a new copy of
the message in the destination folder and removes the original message.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.ReadWrite</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the previously created message entity.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Destination Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the destination folder.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Binary</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Reply All To Message

`<outlook365:reply-all-to-message>`

Replies to all recipients of a message using either JSON or MIME format. The message is then saved
in the Sent Items folder.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the message that replies to all.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body Content Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></li><li><p>MIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type for the request payload.</p></td><td><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message to publish.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Reply To Message

`<outlook365:reply-to-message>`

Replies to the sender of a message using either JSON or MIME format.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the message that replies to all.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mail Folder Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the mail folder that holds the message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body Content Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></li><li><p>MIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type for the request payload.</p></td><td><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message to publish.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST

- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN

- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Send Mail

`<outlook365:send-mail>`

Sends the message specified in the request body. The message is saved in the Sent Items folder by
default. This operation limits the size of the attachment you can add to 3 MB.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message to send.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Body Content Format</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></li><li><p>MIME</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Content type for the request payload.</p></td><td><p>APPLICATION_JSON</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

### Throws

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
- OUTLOOK365:FORBIDDEN
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CONNECTION
- OUTLOOK365:INVALID_CREDENTIALS
- OUTLOOK365:NOT_FOUND
- OUTLOOK365:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- OUTLOOK365:SERVER_ERROR
- OUTLOOK365:TIMEOUT
- OUTLOOK365:VALIDATION

## Send Message

`<outlook365:send-message>`

Sends a message from the draft folder. The draft message can be a new message draft, reply draft,
reply-all draft, or forward draft. The message is then saved in the Sent Items folder.

The following table shows the required permissions for this operation:

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Permission Type</th><th>Permissions (from least privileged to most privileged)</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Delegated (work or school account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Delegated (personal Microsoft account)</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Application</p></td><td><p><code>Mail.Send</code></p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the user who performs the action.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Message Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Message to send.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Read timeout value. Used to override the read timeout values defined in the connector configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Read Timeout Time Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit value for the <strong>Read Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the configuration used to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Outlook365 Config](#Outlook365Config)

- OUTLOOK365:APPLICATION_THROTTLED
- OUTLOOK365:BAD_REQUEST
- OUTLOOK365:CONNECTIVITY
